penalties: Interop risk: treegrid: Treegrid combines tree + grid problems — very low cross-AT support
suggestedFixes: Verify role="treegrid" with target AT/browser pairs; consider a simpler or native pattern if support is inconsistent on critical flows.

evidence.description: Target role, name, and structural position came from the browser accessibility snapshot.

penalties: Interop risk: tree: Tree widget navigation model poorly supported outside of JAWS
suggestedFixes: Verify role="tree" with target AT/browser pairs; consider a simpler or native pattern if support is inconsistent on critical flows.

diagnostics.message: No skip-to-content link found. Keyboard and screen-reader users must Tab through all navigation elements to reach the main content. A skip link is the single most impactful fix for navigation cost. Concrete savings: a skip link would bypass 3 controls (43% of page interactive targets).
No <footer> / contentinfo landmark found. Adding <footer> helps screen-reader users locate site-wide links.
Structural overview: 4 headings · landmarks: main, banner, navigation · missing: contentinfo · skip link: no · 58 total targets

3 text elements lose contrast under simulated color-vision deficiency (tritanopia: 3). Examples (tritanopia): a "Native table" — 9.4:1 normal → 2.44:1 tritanopia (needs 4.5:1); a "ARIA grid" — 9.4:1 normal → 2.44:1 tritanopia (needs 4.5:1); a "Treegrid" — 9.4:1 normal → 2.44:1 tritanopia (needs 4.5:1). Approximately 8% of male and 0.5% of female users have a CVD; choose color combinations whose contrast survives the deuteranopia/protanopia/tritanopia transforms or rely on luminance + non-color cues.
